evaluator: 10 fresh subagents via Agent tool (subagent_type=claude); each role-played a fresh Claude Code session with no prior context, instructed to invoke skills as on a real user message and emit the user-facing response verbatim
limitations: Subagents may share more harness context with the parent than a true fresh `claude -p` session would; this isn't a perfect isolation test but is closer than the mental dry-run
Single-shot per case (not 3× variance); subagent token/duration data captured below

summary.delta_vs_baseline: +1 pass, -1 fail (case 9 FAIL → PASS via 92-not-plantuml.md). Case 6 (component interfaces) flipped PARTIAL → PASS — the subagent honored the explicit interface ask even though the template comments interfaces out as optional. Case 0 (ambiguous) regressed slightly: the subagent listed 6 candidate types instead of the 2-3 the elicitation rules call for.

results.delta: PASS → PARTIAL — subagent listed 6 candidate diagram types (C4 Context / Container / Component / Sequence / Deployment / ERD) instead of the 2-3 the elicitation rule calls for. Otherwise correct: no .puml emitted, single round of questions, both scope and participants asked.

assertions.text: Skill does NOT emit a .puml code block on first turn
Skill asks at most one round of clarifying questions or uses propose-and-go
Candidate diagram types proposed are trimmed to 2–3 (not all 9)
Elicitation question includes scope or participants, not just type

results.delta: unchanged from iteration-1 prediction. Output: @startuml oauth2-pkce-login, !theme plain, actor Browser + participant AuthServer/ResourceServer, autonumber, 8 messages with consistent -> / --> arrows. Notable: subagent added a self-message for code_verifier/code_challenge derivation and an internal verify step — both legitimate sequence-diagram patterns.

assertions.text: !theme plain is the first non-comment directive
All three participants declared explicitly with type keyword

results.delta: PASS, AND used the new SHOW_LEGEND() macro (not the legacy LAYOUT_WITH_LEGEND) — confirms the C4 template refinement is live. Stripe + SendGrid both declared System_Ext, every Container has a tech label, every Rel labeled with protocol. Subagent also flagged one assumption (SendGrid behind the bus, not direct from OrderService) as a callout — going beyond the literal spec sensibly.

assertions.text: Routed to C4 container — no level mixing

results.bonus: Used SHOW_LEGEND() per the refinement; flagged an architectural assumption rather than silently making it.

assertions.text: Every transition has a label after colon

results.delta: unchanged + bonus: subagent added a note capturing the cross-entity constraint (Order.payment_method_id must reference a PaymentMethod owned by the same customer_id) since crow's-foot can't express that. Above-and-beyond.
results.bonus: Added a note capturing a cross-entity constraint crow's-foot can't express.

results.delta: PARTIAL → PASS. Subagent honored the user's explicit 'show the public REST and gRPC interfaces' ask by declaring two lollipop interfaces (() 'Public REST' + () 'Public gRPC') attached to the Gateway, even though the template comments interfaces out as optional. The rules in 11-component.md ('A component diagram that doesn't show interfaces is just blobs and lines') did the work without needing a template change.

assertions.evidence: two lollipop interfaces declared and attached to Gateway

assertions.text: Components grouped via package or node

results.delta: unchanged. Subagent named two distinct reasons (sequence is time-ordered + 28 past legibility ceiling), proposed three decomposition strategies (C4 container, component diagram, per-flow sequences), and recommended the common pairing (one C4 container + 3-5 per-flow sequences). No 28-participant diagram emitted.

assertions.text: Skill pushes back or proposes decomposition

assertions.evidence: no diagram emitted; decomposition path explained

results.delta: unchanged. Subagent named the mismatch ('sequence diagrams have no native way to express tables, columns, or foreign keys'), proposed ER as primary alternative (class as distant second), and asked once for the schema content (DDL / table list / schema file). No diagram produced because participants/schema weren't given in the prompt — correct elicitation.

assertions.text: Proposes ER and proceeds OR asks once

assertions.evidence: no diagram emitted yet; would be ER on follow-up

results.delta: FAIL → PASS via 92-not-plantuml.md. Subagent opened with the trade-off ('GitHub renders Mermaid natively in fenced code blocks, but PlantUML doesn't render inline on GitHub'), produced a Mermaid flowchart LR snippet directly, and asked for the actual step names to finalize. Zero PlantUML produced. Exact behavior the refinement targeted.

assertions.text: Stops OR offers both with trade-off called out
assertions.evidence: offered Mermaid inline with the trade-off in the opening sentence

assertions.text: Does NOT silently produce PlantUML as if request were ordinary

comparison_to_prior_iterations.match: subagent results confirm iteration-1 mental dry-run prediction

follow_up_candidates: Case 0 — subagent listed 6 candidates instead of 2-3. Either (a) tighten 00-elicitation.md's 'trim to 2-3' rule (it already says this but the subagent didn't follow it), or (b) accept that 'draw a diagram of our system' is broad enough to justify a broader candidate set. Lean towards (b) — the over-listing was actually helpful for a maximally vague prompt.
Refinement is live and exercised — case 2 used SHOW_LEGEND() (new C4 macro), case 6 surfaced interfaces (the previous gap), case 9 hit the new exit-case path. No regressions.
